title: Timeless Storage Bed with 12 Drawers
summary: A full storage bed: plywood headboard/footboard panels and two 12-drawer storage bases flanking the mattress — real casework, real drawer slides, no wasted space under the bed.
description:
This is two separate builds joined into one bed: a headboard/footboard pair, and two long storage bases (one per side of the mattress) that carry six drawers each. Treat the storage bases like cabinets on their sides — because that's what they are — and the drawer-slide layout is the part that decides whether the whole thing feels right or fights you every day.

Install the drawer slides before you box in the dividers with plywood, not after. Once the back panel is on and the box is closed up, you're reaching in blind. Inset the slides 3/4" from the front edge of the opening so there's room for a drawer face to sit proud later, and cut every drawer box exactly 1" narrower than its opening — that's the clearance most bottom-mount slides need, and it's unforgiving in both directions: too tight and the drawer binds, too loose and it racks.

The headboard and footboard are the same panel with different leg lengths, so build them side by side and you'll catch mismatches before they're permanent. The moulding detail on the front is optional — skip it for a plainer look, or add it for a piece that reads as furniture instead of a box on legs.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood storage-base sides down to consistent width.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet — ripped into strips
materials.note: Ripped into ~15-3/4"-wide strips for the two storage-base sides and dividers.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood or backerboard, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Storage-base backs — also what squares the boxes up.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Two for headboard/footboard trim, two for mattress slats.

materials.name: Decorative moulding, 8 ft
materials.note: Optional front detail on the headboard/footboard.

cutList.note: Same panel as the headboard, just shorter legs.
Optional. Long point to long point, both ends mitered 45°, not parallel.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: From the 3/4" plywood sheet cut two headboard/footboard panels at 26-3/4" x 36". Rip the two strip sheets to 15-3/4" wide and crosscut four storage base sides at 75" and eight ends/dividers at 14-7/8". From the 1/4" plywood cut two storage base backs at 16-1/4" x 75" and twelve drawer bottoms at 14" x 23". From the Pine 1x3 cut two panel top trims at 36" and 14 drawer support blocks at 7"; that is two 8 ft boards, with two more 1x3 held back for mattress slats. From the Pine 1x2 cut two panel bottom trims at 36" — that is the 1x2's only job, so ONE 8 ft board covers it, not three. The optional mouldings come from the two 8 ft decorative moulding sticks: four long at 30" and four short at 16-3/4", all ends mitered 45° and not parallel, which packs as two long plus two short per stick. Cut two headboard legs at 49-1/4" and two footboard legs at 30-1/4" from the 2x3; four frame rails at 70" and four frame ends at 10" from the 2x4; two mattress cleats at 75" from the 2x2; 24 drawer box sides at 14" and twelve fronts/backs at 21-1/2" from the seven 1x4; and twelve drawer faces at 23-3/4" from the four 1x8.

steps.title: Build the headboard and footboard panels
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around the top and side edges of both plywood panels. Attach the top and bottom trim to the front face, then attach the legs flush to the trim and to the back of the plywood with 1-1/4" pocket screws. The headboard and footboard are the same panel — the footboard just gets the shorter legs, so build both side by side and check them against each other before moving on.

steps.title: Add optional moulding detail
steps.body: If you're adding the moulding, glue and 3/4" brad-nail it to the panel faces now, mitered corners tight. Skip this step entirely for a plainer look — it changes nothing structural.

steps.title: Install drawer slides before boxing in the dividers
steps.body: Lay out each storage base before anything is fastened, because this is the measurement the whole bed turns on. A base is 75" long with a 3/4" end panel at each end and two 3/4" dividers between them, which leaves three bays of exactly 24" — and 24" is why a drawer box built 23" wide is the 1" narrower that bottom-mount slides want. Mount a slide member on each face that borders an opening: the inside face of both ends and BOTH faces of every divider, all of them set back 3/4" from the front edge so an applied drawer face has somewhere to sit. Do this now while the box is open on all sides; once the sides and the plywood back are on you are reaching in blind with a driver you cannot see. Set each pair level to each other with a square and mark the height on both the end and the divider from the same reference edge, or the drawer will run downhill.

steps.title: Assemble the storage-base frames and dividers
steps.body: Build the 2x4 frame for each base from two 70" rails and two 10" ends, then set the plywood sides, the two 14-7/8" end panels and the two 14-7/8" dividers onto it, joining them with pocket holes or 1-1/4" brads and glue. Keep every divider dead square to the sides as you fasten it — a divider that leans takes both of its slides out of parallel and you will feel it in two drawers, not one. Check the base's diagonals with a long tape and hold it square while you glue and brad the 1/4" plywood back on with 3/4" brads: the back is what fixes the box square permanently, so it is the last thing on and it goes on to a box you have just measured, not one you assume. Build the second base the same way and compare the two bay-by-bay before moving on.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Each drawer box is two 14" 1x4 sides running the full depth with a 21-1/2" front and back captured between them, which makes the box exactly 23" wide outside — the 14" x 23" plywood bottoms are the proof of that reading, and 23" in a 24" bay is the 1" of total clearance the slides need. Glue and brad each corner with 1-1/4" brads, then glue and pin the 1/4" bottom onto the underside with 3/4" brads, checking the diagonals of every box before the glue grabs. Measure your actual bays first rather than trusting the numbers blindly: if a bay came out 23-7/8" or 24-1/8", that box needs to follow it, because a bottom-mount slide is unforgiving in both directions — too tight and the drawer binds, too loose and it racks in the opening. Build all twelve to the same setup so they are interchangeable.

steps.title: Hang the drawers and attach faces
steps.body: Fit the drawer member of each slide to its box, slide all twelve boxes home, and check that each runs freely to the back before any face goes on. Then hang the faces: a 23-3/4" face in a 24" opening leaves 1/8" of reveal at each side, so cut spacers to that thickness and rest the face on them while you fix it rather than eyeballing the gap. Fasten from inside the drawer box through its front into the back of the face so nothing shows, using screws short enough not to break through a 3/4" face. Work along one base setting all six faces before you judge them, since the eye reads the whole row of gaps at once and one face 1/16" out is obvious in a line of six. Add the knobs or handles last, marking them all from a single template.

steps.title: Build the drawer support boxes
steps.body: Each loaded storage base carries real weight over a 75" span, so build a support under each one, set flush to the back of the base and to the headboard end, using the 2x4 offcuts and pocket screws. The plan cuts 14 drawer support blocks at 7" from 1x3 scrap but never says where they go; the sensible use is to pack them behind or beneath the slide runs where a base needs bearing, and their placement is genuinely left to you. Set both bases on the floor where the bed will live, get them level with each other, and shim the supports rather than relying on the floor being flat — a base that sits low at one end puts every drawer in it out of level.

steps.title: Assemble the bed
steps.body: Stand the headboard and footboard at the ends of the two storage bases and screw them to the base ends, checking the whole assembly for square across the diagonals before the last screws. Be aware of one number that does not close: the panels are 36" wide and the 2x3 legs add 2-1/2" at each side, so the headboard is 41" across, which is narrower than the mattress plus the two 15-3/4" bases it has to span — measure your own mattress and bases and work out how the headboard meets them before you drill. Screw a 75" 2x2 cleat along the inside face of each base, both at the same height, then cut mattress slats from the two 1x3 held back for the purpose and screw them down across the cleats so they cannot shift; the plan gives no slat length, count or spacing, so size them to the gap between your cleats and space them to whatever your mattress maker specifies. Sand everything, finish, and load the drawers.

images.alt: A platform bed with twelve storage drawers built into two side bases flanking the mattress
